commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a ZAI provider stream wrapper, tests, docs/changelog, package metadata, and lockfile changes to inject `X-Session-Id` from stream options for sticky prompt-cache routing.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defects: source inspection shows the missing session-id path and stale wrapper integration, and Node Headers probes reproduce invalid-value failures and duplicate header normalization.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are localized code, package, and test repairs that an automated repair lane can attempt after rebasing the branch.

target.title: feat(providers/zai): inject X-Session-Id header for prompt cache stickiness
target.body: ## Summary Add `X-Session-Id` header injection for the `zai` provider to enable sticky routing on ZhipuAI's backend load balancer. On a live agent workload (78 GLM-5.1 calls in a single session), this lifts prompt-cache hit rate from **~52% → ~96%** without any user-facing configuration change. Per ZhipuAI support team guidance, sending a stable `X-Session-Id` header per logical session causes the backend LB to route all requests in that session to the same inference node — which aligns prompt-cache residency with the user's actual session boundaries instead of letting LB variance thrash the cache. ## Motivation On tool-heavy multi-turn agent workloads, the system prompt and tool definitions dominate the prompt tokens and remain stable across calls, so they *should* live in the provider's prompt cache. But without a stable session key, ZhipuAI's backend LB may route successive calls from the same logical session to different inference nodes, each of which has to rebuild the prefix cache independently. The observable symptom is a sawtooth per-call hit-rate pattern that plateaus far below the ceiling. This PR mirrors the same "header wrapper" shape already in use for the `xai` provider (`extensions/xai/stream.ts`), so the change is idiomatic and low-risk. ## Design Added `extensions/zai/stream.ts`: - **`createZaiSessionIdHeaderWrapper(baseStreamFn?)`** — stream wrapper that reads `options.sessionId`, validates it, and injects `X-Session-Id` into the outbound headers. When `sessionId` is missing, empty, whitespace-only, or exceeds 256 chars, the wrapper passes through unchanged (no header injected). - **`wrapZaiProviderStream(ctx)`** — provider-level composition. Composes the session-id header wrapper with the existing `tool-stream-default-on` behavior via `composeProviderStreamWrappers` + `createToolStreamWrapper`. Shape mirrors `extensions/xai/stream.ts`. `extensions/zai/index.ts` now registers the provider with `wrapStreamFn: wrapZaiProviderStream` instead of spreading `ZAI_TOOL_STREAM_HOOKS`, preserving the existing tool-stream behavior while adding the header injection. ## Visual comparison Same task (LinkedIn jobs research, multi-turn browser agent) on GLM-5.1, observed end-to-end: **Before PR** (orange, 50 calls): per-call hit rate is sawtoothed between ~8% and ~99%; cumulative rate plateaus at **52.4%**. 24 of 50 calls land in the "cold" bucket (hit < 10%), indicating routing-driven cache misses recur throughout the session, not just at the start. **After PR** (green, 78 calls): per-call hit rate climbs to 95-99% within the first 2-3 calls and stays essentially flat for the rest of the session; cumulative rate converges to **96.27%**. Only **1** cold call (the mandatory first-call warm-up). Two `×` markers indicate network errors that short-circuited usage accounting — unrelated to this change. ## Test plan ### Unit tests (`extensions/zai/stream.test.ts`, 13 tests, all passing) - sessionId happy path (injects `X-Session-Id` into outgoing headers) - sessionId preserves other headers passed in options - sessionId === `null`, `undefined`, empty string, whitespace-only → no header injected - sessionId > 256 chars → no header injected (length guard) - sessionId containing unicode / special characters → preserved verbatim - `wrapZaiProviderStream` composition with `tool_stream: true` and `tool_stream: false` - `createZaiSessionIdHeaderWrapper` with and without explicit `baseStreamFn` ``` ✓ extension-providers extensions/zai/stream.test.ts (13 tests) 16ms Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 13 passed (13) ``` The pre-existing `cache-ttl.test.ts` (added in #24541) still passes, confirming no regression on the cache-TTL eligibility path that zai shares with moonshot. ### Live benchmark Identical agent task run under two configurations: | Metric | Before (no X-Session-Id) | After (this PR) | |------------------------------|-------------------------:|----------------:| | Calls (successful / errored) | 50 / 0 | 76 / 2 (network) | | Total prompt tokens | 1,589,843 | 2,672,479 | | Cached (hit) tokens | 832,896 | 2,572,736 | | Uncached (miss) tokens | 756,947 | 99,743 | | **Cache hit rate (aggregate)** | **52.39%** | **96.27%** | | **Median per-call hit rate** | **79.35%** | **99.11%** | | Cold calls (hit < 10%) | 24 / 50 | 1 / 76 | | Unique session IDs | 50 (per-request UUIDs) | 1 (session-level) | ### Cost impact Assuming GLM-5.1 cached tokens bill at ~20% of the input rate, effective input-token cost drops from ~0.58× nominal to ~0.23× nominal on this workload — **roughly a 60% input-token cost reduction**. ## Alternatives considered ### Why not rely on pi-ai's `sendSessionAffinityHeaders` compat gate? `pi-ai@0.69.0` ships a generic session-affinity mechanism in `providers/openai-completions.js` that, when `compat.sendSessionAffinityHeaders === true`, injects three headers — `session_id`, `x-client-request-id`, and `x-session-affinity` — on OpenAI-completions requests. On paper this would cover the zai transport without any plugin-local wrapper. In practice it doesn't help for this PR: 1. **The gate is default-off and currently unreached.** `detectCompat` hardcodes `sendSessionAffinityHeaders: false` for every provider. Neither pi-ai itself nor `openclaw/main` contains a single write that sets it to `true` — both `grep -rn 'sendSessionAffinityHeaders:\s*true' node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist` and `git grep sendSessionAffinityHeaders origin/main` return no matches. Turning it on for zai would be a separate decision in its own right. 2. **The header names don't match.** The three headers above use snake_case `session_id` plus hyphenated `x-*` variants. ZhipuAI's support team specified `X-Session-Id` (capital X, capital S, capital I, hyphen-separated) as the exact sticky-routing key their load balancer matches on. We can't assume the LB accepts the pi-ai variants. 3. **Scope.** Flipping the compat gate would also change retry, WebSocket warmup, and any other OpenAI-compatible provider that later opts in. Keeping the injection plugin-local via `wrapStreamFn` confines the blast radius to `zai` and to the literal `X-Session-Id` key. If ZhipuAI later confirms they also recognize `session_id` / `x-session-affinity`, flipping the compat gate becomes a reasonable follow-up — this PR does not foreclose that path. ## Scope and caveats - This PR changes behavior only for the `zai` provider. No other provider is touched. - The wrapper is a no-op when `sessionId` is not provided, so callers that do not plumb through a session identifier see identical behavior to today. - Baseline data was captured on a deployment that served GLM-5.1 through an OpenAI-compatible proxy layer. The after-PR run is against `api.z.ai` directly using the bundled `zai` provider. Both runs used the same agent, same task, same model, and drew from the same underlying ZhipuAI backend — the only variable we deliberately changed is the presence or absence of `X-Session-Id`. - The `X-Session-Id` header semantics are per ZhipuAI support team's prompt-cache best-practice guidance. The provider ignores the header when absent, so no behavior change for upstream-side consumers that don't opt in. ## Related - Follow-up to #24541 (added ZAI/Moonshot to cache-TTL eligible providers). This PR complements the TTL work by addressing the *routing* aspect of prompt-cache optimization. - Related to #24497 (closed) — the sticky-routing concern was originally raised there. ## Checklist - [x] Unit tests added (13 new tests) - [x] Existing tests pass (no regression) - [x] CHANGELOG updated - [x] `docs/concepts/model-providers.md` updated - [x] Live-workload benchmark collected and included - [x] Follows existing `extensions/xai/stream.ts` pattern for consistency
